name: Peruvian Montaña
summary: Adapted to the eastern Andean slopes where rainforest meets mountain ecosystems.

name: Pernambuco

name: Punto Rojo
summary: Red psychedelic line

name: Putumayo
summary: Located at the junction of Amazon and Andean ecosystems, highly diverse tropical genetics.

name: Rondônia Frontier
summary: Adapted to frontier agricultural zones bordering deep Amazon rainforest. Strong vigor and humidity tolerance.

name: Salta Feral
summary: Naturalized population, drought tolerant, adapted to large seasonal temperature swings

name: Santa Marta Highlands
region: Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta, Northern Colombia
summary: Home of classic Colombian mountain genetics, adapted to elevation, humidity, and intense sunlight, Colombian Gold origin zone

name: Santa Marta Gold
summary: Legendary Colombian cannabis from the Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta. One of the foundational tropical sativa populations used in modern breeding.

name: Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta
summary: Punto Rojo origin system

name: Suriname
summary: Adapted to hot, wet lowland rainforest conditions. Likely influenced by Caribbean, African, and South American cultivation histories.

name: Ucayali Basin
summary: Adapted to intense humidity, heavy rainfall, and tropical river systems. Potentially one of the richest rainforest cannabis habitats in South America.

name: Ucayali Upper
summary: Upper watershed population distinct from lower Ucayali Basin populations. Adapted to river corridors, seasonal flooding, and rainforest humidity.

name: Vaupés
summary: Indigenous Amazonian cannabis population, exceptional humidity tolerance, highly vigorous growth

name: Venezuela Andes
summary: Adapted to elevation, cooler nights, and strong tropical sunlight. Represents one of northern South America's classic mountain cannabis ecologies.

name: Venezuela Mérida Highlands
summary: Cold-adapted ecotype

name: Yungas
summary: One of Bolivia's most distinctive ecological zones, combining tropical and mountain influences.

name: Zacatecas Purple
summary: Deep purple even in warmth

name: Albania Highlands
summary: Adapted to rugged mountains, limestone soils, and dry summers. Historically isolated from neighboring populations by difficult terrain.

name: Albanian Native
summary: Wild regional population

name: Armenian Highlands
summary: Adapted to high elevations, volcanic soils, cold winters, and dry summers. One of the oldest continuously inhabited agricultural regions in Eurasia.

name: Azerbaijan Lowlands
summary: Adapted to warm summers, fertile floodplains, and Caspian climatic influences. Represents a distinct ecological contrast to the surrounding Caucasus mountain populations.

name: Azores Archipelago
summary: Adapted to strong Atlantic winds, mild temperatures, high humidity, and volcanic soils. Represents one of Europe's most isolated island cannabis ecologies.

name: Balkan Population
summary: Represents the traditional cannabis populations that spread throughout Southeastern Europe via Ottoman, Mediterranean, and Danube trade routes.

name: Balkan Interior Wild
summary: Balkan gene corridor

name: Black Sea Coast
summary: Adapted to high humidity, mild winters, and heavy coastal rainfall. Potential bridge between Caucasus, Anatolian, and Balkan cannabis populations.

name: Bosnia Highlands
summary: Adapted to cold winters, humid summers, and mountainous terrain. Potentially preserves older Balkan hemp and cannabis genetics.

name: Caspian Coast Population
summary: Adapted to salt air, drought, and steppe conditions. Historically linked to Silk Road movement and Caspian trade routes.

name: Cretan
summary: Drought adapted

name: Crimean Population
summary: Crossroads of European and Asian cannabis movement, highly variable regional genetics

name: Danube Basin
summary: One of Europe's oldest hemp cultivation regions, containing diverse naturalized populations.

name: Dnieper Basin
summary: Historic hemp-growing region with long-established feral populations.

name: Don River Basin
summary: Adapted to continental climates and agricultural landscapes of southern Russia.

name: French Corsica Hemp
type: Feral industrial hemp, low THC, high CBD/CBG
summary: Mediterranean feral hemp

name: Georgia Highlands
summary: Adapted to the rugged Caucasus Mountains, cool winters, humid summers, and fertile river valleys. Historically positioned at a crossroads between Europe, Anatolia, Persia, and Central Asia.

name: Hungarian Hemp Landrace
summary: Historic European fiber source, vigorous growth, useful for hemp preservation projects

name: Italian Calabria
summary: Feral European hemp

name: Kalamata
summary: Heat tolerant, narrow-leaf morphology, adapted to dry summers and maritime influence

name: Kalamata Red
summary: Rare southern Greek cannabis population, adapted to hot dry summers and maritime influences, potentially associated with historic hemp and cannabis cultivation. Kalamata, Peloponnese, Greece

name: Kosovo
summary: Adapted to rugged uplands, cold winters, and warm summers. Positioned between Albanian, Serbian, and Macedonian cannabis corridors.

name: Macedonia
summary: Adapted to continental summers, mountain terrain, and Mediterranean influences from the south.

name: Mediterranean Island Population
summary: Adapted to long dry summers, salt exposure, and rocky soils. Historically influenced by maritime trade networks.

name: Montenegro
summary: Adapted to limestone mountains, Adriatic maritime influence, and dry summers. Represents a transition between Balkan and Mediterranean ecologies.

name: Portuguese Algarve
summary: Drought tolerant feral

summary: One of Europe's most important historic hemp gene pools, strong fiber traits and vigor

name: Serbian Feral Population
summary: Descended largely from historic hemp cultivation. Highly adaptable and naturalized throughout agricultural regions and river corridors.

name: Spanish Sierra Nevada
summary: High altitude adaptation

summary: Historic hemp and cannabis corridor connecting Eastern Europe and Central Asia.

summary: Drought and cold resistance

name: Alaskan Wild
summary: Cold feral population

name: Bluefields

summary: Famous Jamaican mountain population, strong vigor, incense-fruit terpene profile, adapted to elevation and humidity, Blue Mountains, Eastern Jamaica

summary: Adapted to heavy rainfall and volcanic mountain terrain.

summary: Small but historically significant Eastern Caribbean population.

summary: Adapted to humid island conditions and seasonal tropical weather patterns.

summary: SE Asia × American hybrid pool. Escaped cultivars, 70s guerrilla

name: Kauai Electric
summary: Rare Hawaiian cannabis associated with Kauai's wet tropical environment. Adapted to volcanic soils, intense sunlight, and year-round growing conditions.

name: Kona Gold
summary: One of Hawaii's most famous heirloom cannabis populations. Adapted to volcanic soils, intense tropical sunlight, oceanic airflow, and year-round growing conditions. Known for vigorous growth, long flowering, and classic tropical fruit, spice, and citrus expressions. Historically associated with the Kona coffee-growing region on the Big Island.

name: Lasqueti Haze
summary: Preservation line associated with British Columbia island culture, selected for cool coastal outdoor conditions.

name: Martinique
summary: French Caribbean island population with strong tropical adaptation and maritime influence.

name: Mexican Sierra Madre Occidental
summary: One of Mexico's largest and most important cannabis-producing mountain systems. Adapted to elevation, seasonal rainfall, and rugged terrain. Source region for many classic Mexican sativas.

name: Midwest Prairie Hemp

summary: Feral hemp population, remnant of WWII fiber hemp programs. High CBD

name: Nevis
summary: Closely related to neighboring island populations but shaped by local island isolation.

name: Puerto Rico Highlands

name: Road Kill Skunk
region: Commonly associated with Northern California and Pacific Northwest preservation circles
summary: Famous "lost skunk" line known for overwhelming skunk-spray aromas, legendary status among preservationists.

summary: Rare New Zealand preservation line believed to have circulated through underground grower networks during the 1970s–1990s. Selected under cool, wet North Island conditions. Known more as a regional heirloom than a commercial cultivar. Likely carries traits for mold resistance, outdoor vigor, and adaptation to variable maritime weather.
